key_insight: The paper shows that a steady-state 2D induction heating model can be rigorously justified and accurately solved with finite elements, even when the source term is only weakly integrable.
engineering_relevance: This supports reliable simulation of induction heating processes in manufacturing and thermal design, especially where preserving physical bounds in temperature is important.
method: Establish weak well-posedness using a priori regularity for the coupled PDEs
Analyze standard Galerkin FEM convergence under convex-domain and mesh assumptions
Apply a bound-preserving method (BPM) to the heat equation for weaker domain/mesh requirements
evidence_signal: The abstract reports several numerical experiments that confirm the theory and demonstrate improved convergence with the bound-preserving method versus standard FEM.
watch_item: The strongest standard FEM convergence result depends on convex domains and suitable meshes, so performance may degrade when geometry or discretization quality is poor.
paper_title: Analysis and approximation of a two-dimensional induction heating problem
